teamNote: A cohesive end-game meta team, not five separate builds. Maelle is the carry; Lune and Monoco generate AP, Breaks and buffs to fuel her; Sciel banks Foretell for a second nuke. You field three at a time — run the core trio (Maelle + Lune + one of Monoco/Sciel) and swap the fourth to the fight. Verso is the flex swap when you want raw aggression over support.
note: Each character shows their part of the team build — best-in-slot weapon, the three defining Pictos (with icons), the Luminas to run, attribute spread and skills. Attribute emphasis uses the in-game 0–99 scale; exact totals depend on level and gear. Pictos double as Luminas you can share across the party.

characters.name: Maelle
characters.teamRole: The win condition. The team exists to hand Maelle AP and Break windows; she converts them into team-wide damage from Virtuose stance.
characters.summary: Stance-dance into Virtuose and unload near-100% crit hits without ever taking damage. Glass Cannon + Immaculate make her a one-woman wipe button — as long as the team keeps her topped up on AP and she keeps parrying.

pictos.name: Immaculate
pictos.effect: Big damage bonus while you've taken no damage this battle — rewards perfect parrying.

pictos.name: Glass Cannon
pictos.effect: 30%+ increased damage, but you can't be healed — pure offense.

pictos.name: Inverted Affinity
pictos.effect: Flips a drawback into an advantage for her scaling — leans all-in.

characters.statsFocus: Stack Critical Rate toward 100% and Speed for extra turns; Health/Defense come from gear, not points.
playstyle: Open in Offensive stance, build to Virtuose, then dump your biggest skills during an extra turn.
Parry everything — Immaculate only keeps your damage maxed while you stay untouched.
Spend the AP the team feeds you; never sit on a full bar.
pros: Snowballs once stances are set up
cons: Depends on the team for AP

characters.name: Lune
characters.teamRole: The engine. Generates AP and elemental Stains, keeps Burn ticking for free damage, and heals/cleanses so the squad never stalls.
characters.summary: Bank elemental Stains to power empowered spells, loop Burn for damage-over-time, and feed the party AP with Energising Parry. Fast, safe and flexible — the glue of the comp.

pictos.name: Energising Parry
pictos.effect: A successful parry grants AP — fuels the whole team's economy.

pictos.name: Charging Critical
pictos.effect: Builds Critical Rate as you act, so your spells start landing crits.

pictos.name: Shortcut
pictos.effect: Reduces the AP cost of skills so you cast more per turn.

characters.statsFocus: Speed first (act often to keep Stains, Burn and AP flowing), then Critical Rate.
playstyle: Generate elemental Stains early, then spend them on empowered spells.
Keep Burn applied for free damage-over-time while the team sets up.
Parry to bank AP for Maelle, and heal/cleanse the squad when needed.
cons: Stain management has a learning curve
Lower personal burst than the carries

characters.name: Sciel
characters.teamRole: The second nuke. While Maelle resets stances, Sciel banks Foretell and detonates for a huge hit — so the team always has burst available.
characters.summary: A slow build to an enormous payoff. Stack Foretell (Sun/Moon) and Dark, weaken enemies, then detonate for a massive hit while scaling through criticals and survivability.

pictos.name: Immaculate
pictos.effect: Big damage bonus while undamaged — pairs with her survivability.

pictos.name: Glass Cannon
pictos.effect: 30%+ damage, no healing — cash in her detonations.

pictos.name: Inverted Affinity
pictos.effect: Turns a drawback into an advantage for her scaling.

characters.statsFocus: Luck for Critical Rate plus Defense to survive the build-up; she scales with crits.
playstyle: Stack Foretell charges (Sun/Moon) over a few turns.
Apply Dark and weaken enemies, then unleash her detonation skills for a massive hit.
Use her survivability so the long set-up never gets interrupted.

characters.name: Monoco
characters.teamRole: The opener. Shreds Break gauges to stagger tough foes for the whole party and refunds AP with Energising Parry — turning hard fights into Maelle windows.
characters.summary: The toolbox. Copies enemy abilities through the Bestial Wheel, breaks staggers, and runs high-Speed parry support to keep AP flowing. Pick the Bestial skills that match the fight.

pictos.name: Energising Parry
pictos.effect: Parrying grants AP — the team's AP battery.

pictos.name: Immaculate
pictos.effect: Damage bonus while undamaged — supported by his parry uptime.

pictos.name: Charging Critical
pictos.effect: Builds Critical Rate as he acts for steadier damage.

characters.statsFocus: Speed above all (act first to set up Break), with Defense to keep him on the field.
playstyle: Pick Bestial Wheel skills that match the fight (Break, damage or utility).
Focus the enemy Break gauge to stagger them for the whole party.
Parry constantly — Energising Parry refunds AP so the team can spend freely.
cons: Reward depends on knowing the fight

characters.name: Verso
characters.teamRole: Aggression swap. Drop in for Monoco or Sciel when a fight rewards pure damage over support — Verso climbs Ranks to S and counters for huge hits.
characters.summary: Lives on the edge. Verso climbs combat Ranks (S being the peak) where his damage explodes, fuelled by parries and counters. Precise and devastating once he's at S-Rank.

pictos.name: Confident Fighter
pictos.effect: 30% increased damage but can't be Healed — leans into the aggression.

pictos.name: Perilous Parry
pictos.effect: Tighter parry window for a much bigger reward — high risk, high payoff.

characters.statsFocus: Critical Rate (Luck) and Speed; he wants to act fast and crit to climb Ranks.
playstyle: Start fights and immediately work up to S-Rank for the damage multiplier.
Parry and counter relentlessly — Augmented Counter is most of his damage.
Don't let your Rank drop; avoid hits that knock you down a tier.
